title: davallia pubescens
intro: Davallia pubescens, also known as the hair fern or rabbit's foot fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Davalliaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, where it is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Davallia pubescens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
